Capítulo 4

Soraya Cerrada Baeza

2026-06-17

Filtrado

Vamos a seleccionar las variables que nos interesan.

library(dplyr)
## Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.2.3
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(stringr)
setwd("~/../Escritorio/TFG/")

datos <- read.delim("state_VT_loan_purposes_1.csv", sep = ",", header = TRUE)

# Sustituir puntos y guiones por barras bajas en los nombres de las variables
names(datos) <- gsub("[.-]", "_", names(datos))

datos_1 <-select(datos,
    debt_to_income_ratio,
    loan_to_value_ratio,
    income,
    applicant_age,
    loan_term,
    action_taken,
    derived_loan_product_type,
    derived_dwelling_category,
    occupancy_type,
    loan_purpose,
    business_or_commercial_purpose,
    loan_type,
    loan_amount,
    interest_rate,
    reverse_mortgage,
    open_end_line_of_credit,
    negative_amortization,
    interest_only_payment,
    balloon_payment,
    other_nonamortizing_features,
    property_value,
    construction_method,
    applicant_credit_score_type,
    co_applicant_credit_score_type)

datos_1
##      debt_to_income_ratio loan_to_value_ratio income applicant_age loan_term

Observamos las frecuencias de algunas variables para poder filtrar posteriormente y quedarnos con aquellos valores con una frecuencia significativamente dominante.

select(datos_1,action_taken,derived_loan_product_type,derived_dwelling_category,occupancy_type,loan_purpose,loan_type,reverse_mortgage,co_applicant_credit_score_type,business_or_commercial_purpose,open_end_line_of_credit,negative_amortization,interest_only_payment,balloon_payment,other_nonamortizing_features,construction_method) %>%
  
mutate(across(everything(), as.factor)) %>%  # Las convierto en factor para que summary calcule frecuencias y no medias
summary()
##   action_taken                  derived_loan_product_type
##  1      :5861   Conventional:First Lien      :7567       
##  4      :1306   Conventional:Subordinate Lien: 354       
##  6      : 923   FHA:First Lien               : 689       
##  3      : 708   FSA/RHS:First Lien           :  65       
##  5      : 210   FSA/RHS:Subordinate Lien     :  35       
##  2      : 166   VA:First Lien                : 520       
##  (Other):  56                                            
##                           derived_dwelling_category occupancy_type loan_purpose
##  Multifamily:Manufactured              :   3        1:7477         1:9230      
##  Multifamily:Site-Built                :  65        2:1066                     
##  Single Family (1-4 Units):Manufactured: 564        3: 687                     
##  Single Family (1-4 Units):Site-Built  :8598                                   
##                                                                                
##                                                                                
##                                                                                
##  loan_type reverse_mortgage co_applicant_credit_score_type
##  1:7921    1   :   4        9      :3872                  
##  2: 689    2   :8919        10     :3115                  
##  3: 520    1111: 307        1      : 665                  
##  4: 100                     3      : 620                  
##                             2      : 464                  
##                             1111   : 307                  
##                             (Other): 187                  
##  business_or_commercial_purpose open_end_line_of_credit negative_amortization
##  1   : 554                      1   : 285               1   :   1            
##  2   :8369                      2   :8638               2   :8922            
##  1111: 307                      1111: 307               1111: 307            
##                                                                              
##                                                                              
##                                                                              
##                                                                              
##  interest_only_payment balloon_payment other_nonamortizing_features
##  1   : 362             1   : 190       1   :   4                   
##  2   :8561             2   :8733       2   :8919                   
##  1111: 307             1111: 307       1111: 307                   
##                                                                    
##                                                                    
##                                                                    
##                                                                    
##  construction_method
##  1:8663             
##  2: 567             
##                     
##                     
##                     
##                     
##

Una vez estudiadas las frecuencias hacemos el primer filtrado.

datos_2 <- filter(datos_1,
    action_taken == 1,                                            # Préstamo concedido
    derived_loan_product_type == "Conventional:First Lien",       # Hipoteca estándar 
    derived_dwelling_category == "Single Family (1-4 Units):Site-Built",    # Vivienda unifamiliar 
    occupancy_type == 1,                                          # Vivienda habitual
    loan_type == 1,                                               # Préstamo convencional privado
    reverse_mortgage == 2,                                        # NO es hipoteca inversa
    co_applicant_credit_score_type == 9,                          # Sin co-solicitante 
    business_or_commercial_purpose == 2,                          # Préstamo no comercial
    open_end_line_of_credit == 2,                                 # No es una línea de crédito abierta
    negative_amortization == 2,                             #Sin amortización negativa 
    interest_only_payment == 2,                             #Pagos no solo de intereses 
    balloon_payment == 2,                                   #Sin pago global     
    other_nonamortizing_features == 2,            #Otras características que no se amortizan completamente                 
    construction_method == 1                                #De obra, no prefabricada          
  )

Nos aseguramos de que ninguna de las variables que vamos a utilizar en nuestro modelo toma algún tipo de valor que signifique exento o similar, en caso de hacerlo lo convertimos en NA.

select(datos_2,interest_rate,loan_amount,property_value,loan_to_value_ratio,loan_term,income,debt_to_income_ratio,applicant_age) %>%
  
mutate(across(everything(), as.factor)) %>%  # Las convierto en factor para que summary calcule frecuencias y no medias
summary()
##  interest_rate  loan_amount  property_value loan_to_value_ratio loan_term
##  6.625  : 91   345000 : 25   295000 : 24    80      : 93        180: 20  
##  6.875  : 89   255000 : 24   465000 : 21    95      : 59        240:  9  
##  6.500  : 52   235000 : 23   355000 : 19    90      : 52        360:713  
##  6.5    : 46   305000 : 23   285000 : 18    80.00000: 40                 
##  7.125  : 43   325000 : 23   365000 : 18    97      : 30                 
##  6.125  : 41   405000 : 23   425000 : 17    75.00000: 19                 
##  (Other):380   (Other):601   (Other):625    (Other) :449                 
##      income    debt_to_income_ratio applicant_age
##  105    :  9   20%-<30%:168         <25  : 36    
##  130    :  9   30%-<36%:132         >74  : 11    
##  139    :  9   <20%    : 49         25-34:265    
##  146    :  9   39      : 35         35-44:188    
##  128    :  8   48      : 35         45-54:116    
##  (Other):689   41      : 33         55-64: 86    
##  NA's   :  9   (Other) :290         65-74: 40

Nos aseguramos de tener en cuenta todos los valores que toman las dos variables que tenemos que convertir a numéricas.

table(datos_2$applicant_age)
## 
##   <25   >74 25-34 35-44 45-54 55-64 65-74 
##    36    11   265   188   116    86    40
table(datos_2$debt_to_income_ratio)
## 
##     <20% 20%-<30% 30%-<36%       36       37       38       39       40 
##       49      168      132       25       27       30       35       19 
##       41       42       43       44       45       46       47       48 
##       33       27       27       33       19       30       22       35 
##       49  50%-60% 
##       28        3

Tras filtrar, generamos una base de datos en las que solo aparezcan las variables de nuestro modelo y además convertimos las variables categóricas a numéricas.

library(dplyr)

datos_modelo <- mutate(datos_2,
    interest_rate       = as.numeric(interest_rate),
    loan_amount         = as.numeric(loan_amount),
    property_value      = as.numeric(property_value),
    loan_to_value_ratio = as.numeric(loan_to_value_ratio),
    loan_term           = as.numeric(loan_term),
    income              = as.numeric(income),
    

    debt_to_income_ratio_num = case_when(
      debt_to_income_ratio == "<20%"     ~ 15,
      debt_to_income_ratio == "20%-<30%" ~ 25,
      debt_to_income_ratio == "30%-<36%" ~ 33,
      debt_to_income_ratio == "50%-60%"  ~ 55,
      TRUE                               ~ as.numeric(debt_to_income_ratio)
    ),
    
    applicant_age_num = case_when(
      applicant_age == "<25"   ~ 21,  # Estimación razonable para menores de 25
      applicant_age == "25-34" ~ 30,  # Punto medio de (25+34)/2
      applicant_age == "35-44" ~ 40,  # Punto medio de (35+44)/2
      applicant_age == "45-54" ~ 50,  # Punto medio de (45+54)/2
      applicant_age == "55-64" ~ 60,  # Punto medio de (55+64)/2
      applicant_age == "65-74" ~ 70,  # Punto medio de (65+74)/2
      applicant_age == ">74"   ~ 78,  # Estimación razonable para mayores de 74
      TRUE                     ~ as.numeric(applicant_age)
    )
  ) %>%

  filter(
    !is.na(interest_rate),
    !is.na(loan_amount),
    !is.na(property_value),
    !is.na(loan_to_value_ratio),
    !is.na(loan_term),
    !is.na(income),
    !is.na(debt_to_income_ratio_num),
    !is.na(applicant_age_num)
  )%>%
  # Nos quedamos con las siguientes variables
  select(
    interest_rate,
    loan_amount,
    property_value,
    loan_to_value_ratio,
    debt_to_income_ratio_num,
    income,
    applicant_age_num,    
    loan_term
  )
## Warning: There were 2 warnings in `mutate()`.
## The first warning was:
## ℹ In argument: `debt_to_income_ratio_num = case_when(...)`.
## Caused by warning:
## ! NAs introducidos por coerción
## ℹ Run ]8;;ide:run:dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()]8;; to see the 1 remaining warning.
summary(datos_modelo)
##  interest_rate     loan_amount      property_value    loan_to_value_ratio
##  Min.   : 4.500   Min.   :  45000   Min.   :  65000   Min.   : 11.18     
##  1st Qu.: 6.375   1st Qu.: 245000   1st Qu.: 315000   1st Qu.: 71.99     
##  Median : 6.625   Median : 345000   Median : 455000   Median : 80.00     
##  Mean   : 6.715   Mean   : 384700   Mean   : 524222   Mean   : 78.26     
##  3rd Qu.: 7.000   3rd Qu.: 455000   3rd Qu.: 615000   3rd Qu.: 90.00     
##  Max.   :10.000   Max.   :2105000   Max.   :4305000   Max.   :103.06     
##  debt_to_income_ratio_num     income     applicant_age_num   loan_term    
##  Min.   :15.00            Min.   :  39   Min.   :21.00     Min.   :180.0  
##  1st Qu.:25.00            1st Qu.: 110   1st Qu.:30.00     1st Qu.:360.0  
##  Median :36.00            Median : 148   Median :40.00     Median :360.0  
##  Mean   :35.08            Mean   : 182   Mean   :41.55     Mean   :353.9  
##  3rd Qu.:43.00            3rd Qu.: 209   3rd Qu.:50.00     3rd Qu.:360.0  
##  Max.   :55.00            Max.   :1468   Max.   :78.00     Max.   :360.0

##Análisis descriptivo de interest_rate

library(ggplot2)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.2.2
ggplot(datos_modelo, aes(x = interest_rate)) + 
  geom_histogram(fill = "royalblue", color = "black", bins = 30, alpha = 0.85) + 
  labs(title = "Distribución del Tipo de Interés Nominal en Vermont", x = "Tipo de Interés (%)", y = "Frecuencia") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, hjust = 0.5))

##Modelos

# Modelo regresión lineal
modelo_lineal <- lm(interest_rate ~ loan_amount + property_value + loan_to_value_ratio + debt_to_income_ratio_num + income + applicant_age_num + loan_term, data = datos_modelo)
summary(modelo_lineal)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = interest_rate ~ loan_amount + property_value + loan_to_value_ratio + 
##     debt_to_income_ratio_num + income + applicant_age_num + loan_term, 
##     data = datos_modelo)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.5322 -0.3199 -0.0199  0.2862  3.2271 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)               5.420e+00  2.553e-01  21.234  < 2e-16 ***
## loan_amount              -5.640e-08  2.275e-07  -0.248  0.80429    
## property_value           -3.059e-07  1.418e-07  -2.157  0.03130 *  
## loan_to_value_ratio       8.264e-04  1.749e-03   0.472  0.63673    
## debt_to_income_ratio_num  7.168e-03  2.237e-03   3.205  0.00141 ** 
## income                    4.999e-04  2.090e-04   2.392  0.01700 *  
## applicant_age_num        -5.484e-04  1.495e-03  -0.367  0.71386    
## loan_term                 3.087e-03  6.256e-04   4.934 9.98e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.5085 on 725 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.08968,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.08089 
## F-statistic:  10.2 on 7 and 725 DF,  p-value: 3.177e-12
library(quantreg)
## Warning: package 'quantreg' was built under R version 4.2.3
## Loading required package: SparseM
## 
## Attaching package: 'SparseM'
## The following object is masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     backsolve
cuantiles <- c(0.10, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90)

# Modelo de regresión cuantílica
# Method="br" para utilizar el método desarrollado en el capítulo 3.

modelo_cuantilica <- rq(interest_rate ~ loan_amount + property_value + loan_to_value_ratio + debt_to_income_ratio_num + income + applicant_age_num + loan_term, data = datos_modelo, tau = cuantiles, method = "br")

#Utilizamos se="nid" para que calcule también los p-valores.
summary(modelo_cuantilica, se="nid")
## Warning in summary.rq(xi, U = U, ...): 2 non-positive fis
